name: Burr (DAGWorks State Machine Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); Burr UI is open source; DAGWorks cloud optional

auth.notes: Self-hosted; no auth for OSS version; DAGWorks cloud requires account

performance.notes: Framework overhead minimal; state machine transitions are fast

agent_readiness.notes: State machine framework for building AI agents and LLM applications. Define actions (functions) and transitions. Built-in persistence for long-running stateful workflows. LLM streaming support. OpenTelemetry/tracing built-in. Local UI for visualizing state machines and runs. Integrates with LangChain, OpenAI, Anthropic. Designed specifically for multi-turn conversations and agentic workflows where state persists across steps.

gotchas: State machine paradigm requires conceptual shift
Limited third-party integrations vs LangChain ecosystem
DAGWorks cloud features require paid plan
best_when: Multi-turn conversational AI agents with persistent state
AI applications where you need explicit state management
avoid_when: Teams needing LangChain's extensive tool ecosystem
summary: Burr is a state machine framework from DAGWorks for building stateful AI agents and LLM applications. Define actions and transitions with built-in persistence, streaming, and tracing. Local UI for visualizing agent state. Best for multi-turn conversational agents and long-running workflows where explicit state management matters. More debuggable alternative to LangGraph for state machine patterns.
